{{Quote|A hundred years ago an aristocrat named Baron Yorn left Coruscant and came here with some followers.|A stormtrooper to another stormtrooper|<ref name="Resurrection" />}}
[[Baron/Legends|Baron]] '''Yorn''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Noble/Legends|noble]] from the<ref name="Resurrection" /> [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. According to the legend,<ref name="Resurrection" /> around [[100 BBY/Legends|100 BBY]],<ref name="Date">The events of "[[Resurrection (comic story)|Resurrection]]" take place before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], and after the theft of the [[Death Star plans/Legends|Death Star plans]] by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], which took place during the [[Battle of Toprawa]] per ''[[Jedi Dawn]]''. Since the novel [[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] establishes that the latter battle took place following the [[attack on the first Death Star]], which ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' in turn dates to 0 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]], the Battle of Toprawa must have also occured in 0 BBY, meaning the events of "Resurrection" are set in that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], as well. Since "Resurrection" states that the fortress was built 100 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the events of the comic, it must have been built around [[100 BBY/Legends|100 BBY]].</ref> Yorn brought several followers to the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Kalakar Six]]<ref name="Resurrection" /> in the [[Dromund system]].<ref name="Silent City">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Archaeology|A Silent City}}</ref> The moon's [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] nature attracted the baron and his entourage, who set up a [[Unidentified fortress|fortress]] with the intent to conduct experiments there. However, when a supply [[Starship/Legends|ship]] arrived on the moon, the crew noted that everyone there had vanished, except for Yorn, who had gone mad, tearing out his own [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and [[Tongue/Legends|tongue]].<ref name="Resurrection" />

In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and two [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] came to his long-abandoned fortress to look for the [[Death Star plans/Legends|Death Star plans]]. A [[Unidentified stormtrooper 1 (Kalakar Six)|stormtrooper]] told the other fellow [[Unidentified stormtrooper 2 (Kalakar Six)|stormtrooper]] about Yorn's legend.<ref name="Resurrection" />

==Behind the scenes==
Yorn was mentioned in the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] story "[[Resurrection (comic story)|Resurrection]]," published in the [[Star Wars Tales 9|ninth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'' comic series by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="Resurrection" /> on [[October 10]], [[2001]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/53987/star_wars_tales_1999_9|text=Star Wars Tales (1999) #9}}</ref> The comic was written by [[Ron Marz]].<ref name="Resurrection" />
